sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Foot pain following a fall.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports that he injured his right foot yesterday after dropping a landscape brick on it. He initially felt some soreness, but it has become significantly more painful, accompanied by swelling. He attempted to continue working, but found it difficult to weight-bear. He called a few names and stated that he felt like god damn why is this in his foot. He also mentioned a previous ankle injury twenty years ago that occasionally causes soreness and swelling, and a prior surgery on that ankle. He reports numbness in his foot and occasional tingling. He has taken ibuprofen for the pain but no other medications.
Findings
Physical examination reveals bruising on the bottom and top of the right foot, along with significant swelling. Palpation elicits tenderness at the midfoot. Neurological exam reveals brisk capillary refill in all toes and a strong dorsalis pedis pulse. Motor and sensory function are intact. An X-ray reveals a dorsal displacement of the base of the second metatarsal with a 3mm separation of the first and second metatarsal bases and presence of bony fragments.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient presents with a lisfranc fracture, characterized by dorsal displacement of the second metatarsal and separation of the first and second metatarsal bases, with associated bony fragments. This injury is likely to result in significant foot instability and potential long-term complications.
Plan
We are going to order an MRI to assess ligament injuries. Surgery is planned for tomorrow to align the bones and ligaments. You will be discharged home that evening. You will be in a cast and using crutches for six to eight weeks. We will advance your ambulation gradually based on your healing and tolerance. A follow-up appointment is scheduled in two weeks. The good news is we have an outpatient mri facility downstairs and I'm going to send the order down and we'll get you your mri this afternoon.
